perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and Recipient’s duty to protect Confidential Information expires the longer of three (3) years from the date set forth above or after the conclusion of the Decommissioning of SONGS is completed , unless the Parties agree in writing to a different term .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and Recipient’s duty to protect Confidential Information expires the shorter of three (3) years from the date set forth above or after the conclusion of the Decommissioning of SONGS is completed , unless the Parties agree in writing to a different term .
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in Section 2 concerning the duration of the confidentiality obligations. Originally, the confidentiality lasted the *longer* of two periods. Now, it lasts the *shorter* of the two, potentially ending the confidentiality prematurely. This undermines the overall intent of the agreement, in particularly with Section 1 where it defines the use of Confidential Information for the parties and their obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information. The Parties ag ree that they will not use or disclose (and they will cause their respective employees, agents, affiliates, representatives and consultants not to use or disclose ) in any form or for any reason any of the Confidential Information for any purpose that is not directly related to the Purpose described above. The Party receiving Confidential Information (“Recipient”) shall make use of the Confidential Information only for the Purpose and, except as provided below, shall not provide the Confidential Information to any other person without the express written consent of the party disclosing the Confidential Information ( “Disclosing Party ”). Recipient will notify each emp loyee, agent, affiliate , representative, or consultant involved in the business discussions or who may have any occasion to view, handle, or obtain any of the Confidential Information, of the terms of this Agreement and shall require such person to enter i nto an agreement with terms similar to this Agreement . The Parties further agree that such Confidential Information will only be provided by Recipient to those of its directors, officers, elected or appointed officials , employees, agents, affiliates , advis ors, representatives, or consultants with a need to know.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the duration of the confidentiality obligation from the 'longer' to the 'shorter' of two periods. This directly contradicts the purpose outlined in the contradicted section (Use of Confidential Information) because it potentially shortens the time during which the parties are obligated to protect confidential information. The original text provided a minimum duration, while the changed text provides a maximum, which undermines the agreement's intent to safeguard confidential information and creates a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized use, disclosure, dissemination, or publication of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using a minimal degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized use, disclosure, dissemination, or publication of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the standard of care from 'the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care' to 'a minimal degree of care', the contract introduces a substantial contradiction in Section 3. This directly conflicts with the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 1. It now leaves it open to the interpretation of what *minimal degree of care* actually means in this context, so it is now contradicting the spirit of confidentiality agreed upon.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information. The Parties ag ree that they will not use or disclose (and they will cause their respective employees, agents, affiliates, representatives and consultants not to use or disclose ) in any form or for any reason any of the Confidential Information for any purpose that is not directly related to the Purpose described above. The Party receiving Confidential Information (“Recipient”) shall make use of the Confidential Information only for the Purpose and, except as provided below, shall not provide the Confidential Information to any other person without the express written consent of the party disclosing the Confidential Information ( “Disclosing Party ”). Recipient will notify each emp loyee, agent, affiliate , representative, or consultant involved in the business discussions or who may have any occasion to view, handle, or obtain any of the Confidential Information, of the terms of this Agreement and shall require such person to enter i nto an agreement with terms similar to this Agreement . The Parties further agree that such Confidential Information will only be provided by Recipient to those of its directors, officers, elected or appointed officials , employees, agents, affiliates , advis ors, representatives, or consultants with a need to know.
perturbation.justification: The changed text lowers the standard of care for protecting confidential information to a 'minimal degree of care,' which directly contradicts the original text's requirement of 'the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care.' This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which emphasizes the importance of protecting Confidential Information and requires employees to enter agreements with terms similar to this agreement. The reduced standard of care makes the original intent of the confidentiality clause unenforceable, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient’s obligation shall only extend to Confidential I nformation that: (a) is marked as confidential at the time of disclosure; or (b) is unmarked (e.g. orally disclosed) but treated as Confidential Information at the time of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient’s obligation shall only extend to Confidential I nformation that: (a) is marked as confidential at the time of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes subsection (b) from Section 4, thereby excluding unmarked (e.g., orally disclosed) information from being considered confidential. It creates contradiction with the second 'WHEREAS' paragraph on Page 1, which specifically includes 'certain oral' information in the definition of Confidential Information. Therefore, this removes this clause and goes against the introduction set at page 1 of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, in the course of fulfilling the Purpose, the Parties may disclose to each other confidential and proprietary information, including but not limited to certain oral, written, electronic mail (e -mail), or computer information, documents, materials, models, analyses , and business plans concerning the types of information set forth in Anaheim ’s RFP such as Anaheim ’s residual operating , environmental, marine, and decommissioning liabilities , Anaheim ’s decommissioning trust fund balance, and the nature of SONGS governance rights pursuant to operating, settlement, and decommissioning agreements and balance sheet and financial track record information as well as other information and materials ; the Parties shall treat such confidential and proprietary information as confidential (“Confidential Information”) pursuant to the terms of this A greement; and
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for unmarked (orally disclosed) information to be considered confidential under subsection (b). The changed text removes this subsection, now requiring all confidential information to be marked as confidential at the time of disclosure. This creates a direct contradiction with the 'WHEREAS' paragraph, which explicitly includes 'certain oral' information in the definition of Confidential Information. Therefore, by removing subsection (b), the changed text now contradicts the 'WHEREAS' paragraph on Page 1, which specifically includes unmarked, oral information as Confidential Information. This is a strong contradiction because the changed text now excludes information that the 'WHEREAS' clause explicitly includes, impacting the scope of confidentiality within the document.

perturbation.original_text: The s upplying of Confidential Information shall not be considered to provide any license or proprietary rights, including any implied patent license or intellectual property rights in the property of the other by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The s upplying of Confidential Information shall be considered to provide a limited license to use the Confidential Information internally, but not to create derivative works or transfer the information to third parties. This does not grant any proprietary rights, including any implied patent license or intellectual property rights in the property of the other by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 6 explicitly states that supplying confidential information provides no license or proprietary rights. The modified version now states that it *does* provide a limited license to use the information internally. This directly contradicts the original intent and creates ambiguity about the scope of permitted use, in particular within Section 1 where the Recipient cannot use the Confidential information for any purpose not directly related to the Purpose described in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information. The Parties ag ree that they will not use or disclose (and they will cause their respective employees, agents, affiliates, representatives and consultants not to use or disclose ) in any form or for any reason any of the Confidential Information for any purpose that is not directly related to the Purpose described above. The Party receiving Confidential Information (“Recipient”) shall make use of the Confidential Information only for the Purpose and, except as provided below, shall not provide the Confidential Information to any other person without the express written consent of the party disclosing the Confidential Information ( “Disclosing Party ”). Recipient will notify each emp loyee, agent, affiliate , representative, or consultant involved in the business discussions or who may have any occasion to view, handle, or obtain any of the Confidential Information, of the terms of this Agreement and shall require such person to enter i nto an agreement with terms similar to this Agreement . The Parties further agree that such Confidential Information will only be provided by Recipient to those of its directors, officers, elected or appointed officials , employees, agents, affiliates , advis ors, representatives, or consultants with a need to know.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits granting any license via the provision of Confidential Information, while the changed text explicitly grants a limited license to use the information internally. This directly contradicts the original intention. The contradicted text states that confidential information can only be used for the 'Purpose described above', but now the changed text introduces a 'limited license' which is inconsistent. The changed text gives a limited license when the contradicted text only allows use related to the Purpose.

perturbation.original_text: In no event shall either P arty be liable to the other for consequential, special, indirect, incidental, punitive or exemplary loss, damage , or expense relating to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In no event shall either P arty be liable to the other for consequential, special, indirect, incidental, punitive or exemplary loss, damage , or expense relating to this Agreement, unless such loss, damage or expense is a direct result of intentional misconduct or gross negligence.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception to the waiver of liability in Section 11. The original text provides a blanket waiver, while the modified version introduces liability for 'intentional misconduct or gross negligence.' This directly contradicts the blanket waiver, creating a contradiction to the waiver of liability in section 11 and in turn conflicts with the remedy stated in Section 9, about seeking remedies in law or equity in the event of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledge s and agree s that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party would be irreparably and immediately harmed and could not be made whole by monetary damages. It is accordingly agreed that Disclosing Party, in addition to any other remed y to which it may be entitled in law or equity, shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to compel specific performance of this Agreement, without the need for proof of actual damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a blanket waiver of liability, stating that neither party shall be liable for certain types of losses or damages. The changed text introduces an exception to this waiver, making a party liable for losses or damages resulting from 'intentional misconduct or gross negligence.' The contradicted text states that in the event of a breach, monetary damages would not make the Disclosing Party whole, and it would be entitled to injunctive relief in addition to any other remedy in law or equity. The introduction of liability for intentional misconduct or gross negligence in the changed text directly contradicts the blanket waiver in the original text and conflicts with the clause stating that the party could not be made whole by monetary damages in the contradicted text, because it is now implying that monetary damages *can* apply.
